title: Recognize when `cond` is still the right shape
prompt:
Given:

```
def tax_bracket(income) do
  cond do
    income < 10_000 -> 0.0
    income < 40_000 -> 0.12
    income < 85_000 -> 0.22
    income < 160_000 -> 0.24
    true -> 0.32
  end
end
```

This function is correctly expressed as a `cond` — the ordered, open-ended range checks can't become multi-clause function heads without awkward guard repetition. Keep it as a `cond` but replace the `true ->` fallback with a guard-style clause that makes the terminal case explicit: `income >= 160_000 -> 0.32`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Tax do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Tests the 'don't mechanically convert' judgment. Sonnet typically tries to convert to function heads or removes the cond entirely.
